2606.23271.title: Scaling LLM Knowledge Boundaries via Distribution-Optimized Synthesis
2606.23271.paper_summary: Knowledge injection via synthetic data is crucial for enhancing Large Language Models (LLMs). However, current synthesis methods simply stop at preset token counts or fixed data ratios, lacking awareness of knowledge distribution. This results in some domains being sparse while others are redundant, limiting LLM knowledge boundaries. We revisit knowledge injection from a distribution perspective and hypothesize that an optimal knowledge distribution exists to maximize knowledge boundary expansion. We propose KDoS (Knowledge Distribution-optimized Synthesis), a framework that introduces knowledge density to drive synthesis through a three-stage feedback mechanism, shifting from blind generation to distribution-optimized synthesis. We construct Wikipedia-based synthetic data with varying knowledge distributions and conduct experiments on models from 0.6B to 16B (Qwen, Ling, LLaMA) and data scales from 1B to 5B tokens. Our key findings are: (1) an optimal knowledge distribution consistently maximizes boundary expansion; (2) this distribution is stable across backbones and scales; (3) KDoS outperforms baselines across six knowledge benchmarks. Our work offers a new perspective and practical framework for synthetic data-driven knowledge injection.
2606.23271.paper_summary_zh: 知識注入透過合成數據對於增強大型語言模型（LLMs）至關重要。然而，當前的合成方法僅僅停留在預設的標記數量或固定的數據比例上，缺乏對知識分佈的認識。這導致某些領域的知識稀疏，而其他領域則冗餘，限制了LLM的知識邊界。我們從分佈的角度重新審視知識注入，並假設存在一個最佳的知識分佈，以最大化知識邊界的擴展。我們提出KDoS（知識分佈優化合成），這是一個引入知識密度以推動合成的框架，通過三階段的反饋機制，從盲目生成轉向分佈優化的合成。我們構建了基於維基百科的合成數據，具有不同的知識分佈，並對從0.6B到16B（Qwen、Ling、LLaMA）以及數據規模從1B到5B標記的模型進行實驗。我們的主要發現是：（1）最佳的知識分佈始終最大化邊界擴展；（2）這種分佈在不同的骨幹和規模中是穩定的；（3）KDoS在六個知識基準上超越了基線。我們的工作為基於合成數據的知識注入提供了一個新的視角和實用框架。
2606.23271.author: Songze Li et.al.
2606.23271.authors: Songze Li, Yarong Lan, Zhongpu Bo, Zhaoyang Wang, Zhiqiang Liu, Yuan Yuan, Chengtao Gan, Menghao Qian, Enpei Niu, Xiaoke Guo, Yuanxiang Liu, Zhaoyan Gong, Xiangjin Hu, Liangyurui Liu, Jingdian Lu, Lei Liang, Jun Zhou, Huajun Chen, Wen Zhang
